스시 Sushi Kim 2

Our Story 우리 이야기

A family kitchen,
twenty-five years
in downtown 피츠버그.

Hanji · Drop in

In 2001, the Kim family unpacked a single stone bibimbap bowl on the kitchen counter at 110 Smithfield St. The bowl is still on the pass today — and the recipes haven't changed.

We opened as a neighborhood Korean kitchen for a downtown that didn't yet have one. Our regulars came for galbi tang on cold afternoons, stayed for the banchan, and started bringing their families. Twenty-five years later, the same families are still here — and their kids are here too.

Along the way we built a sushi bar. Our chefs cut by hand every morning, working with the day's best catch. Korean food on one side of the menu, Japanese on the other, both served with the same patience.

How we cook

Three things
we don't compromise.

01
정성

Jeongseong

Cooking with care. Every stew is started in the morning; every piece of fish is cut by hand. The slow way is the only way.

02
가족

Gajok

Family. The Kims still run the kitchen. Our staff have been here for a decade on average.

03
신선

Shinseon

Freshness. Fish arrives daily. Kimchi is fermented in our own jars. Banchan changes with the season.

Timeline

Twenty-five
years, in brief.

2001

Doors open

Sushi Kim opens on Smithfield St. with a six-seat sushi bar and a handwritten Korean menu.

2008

The galbi recipe is set

After seven years of iteration, the galbi marinade is finalized. It has not been changed since.

2013

Second-generation chefs join the kitchen

The Kim children join the line, trained by their parents since childhood.

2019

Rebuild and renovation

The dining room is renovated. The stone bibimbap bowl is kept.

2023

4.8 stars, 200+ reviews

Pittsburgh keeps coming back. We try to earn it every day.

2026

Twenty-five years

Still at 110 Smithfield. Still family-run. Still cooking slow.

Come eat with us

The table's already set.

Sushi Kim 2
